steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for a choice that emphasizes a similarity between Morris and Snethlage. Morris is an entomologist (studies insects); Snethlage is an ornithologist (studies birds). The correct choice must connect them through something they share, not contrast them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "The entomologist Margaretta Morris was born in 1797, but ornithologist Emilie Snethlage was born later, in 1868.": highlights a difference (birth years) and uses but to signal contrast. ✗
  • "Like entomologist Margaretta Morris, ornithologist Emilie Snethlage dedicated her scientific career to the study of living organisms.": the word Like signals similarity, and 'study of living organisms' is the common ground shared by entomology (insects) and ornithology (birds). ✓
  • "Entomology and ornithology are just two examples of the myriad fields in which scientists can specialize.": makes a general statement about scientific fields and does not emphasize any similarity between Morris and Snethlage as individuals. ✗
  • "Margaretta Morris conducted research on wheat flies and cicadas; Emilie Snethlage, by contrast, is known for her research on Brazilian birds.": the phrase by contrast explicitly signals a difference, not a similarity. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
